content.text: All six files have been read. Now I need to also check the `responses.ts` shared file to understand how `corsResponse`, `successResponse`, and `errorResponse` work, since the CORS change will affect those.

I see that `responses.ts` imports `corsHeaders` as an object (not a function), so I need to update it to work with the new function-based `corsHeaders`. Since the responses utility functions don't have access to the request origin, I need to think about how to handle this properly. The `corsHeaders` function now takes an `origin` parameter. I'll need to update `responses.ts` to accept an optional origin, and update the callers accordingly.

Let me also check how `corsHeaders` and `corsResponse` are used across the edge functions.

I see that `responses.ts` uses `corsHeaders` as a static object throughout. The new `corsHeaders` is now a function. I need to update `responses.ts` to work with the new function-based API. The cleanest approach: have `responses.ts` accept an optional origin parameter and call `corsHeaders(origin)`. But since most callers don't pass the origin, I'll provide a default export that works as a function and update `responses.ts` accordingly.

Let me now apply all the fixes. I'll start with FIX 1 (CORS) and the necessary update to `responses.ts`, then proceed to the rest.
